summary: Frontend testing completed for Oryno platform UI improvements (iteration 62). All 6 requested features verified and passed.

minor.issue: Mixed content error - HTTP request from HTTPS page blocked (not related to this iteration)

features_tested.details: Modern warm design with bg-[#f0ebe3] background, no promo code section visible, button shows 'Final Step' (not 'Proceed to Booking'), 'You will not be charged yet' text visible under button

PaymentMethodsSelection.jsx only renders <method.icon /> which contains only the logo images. No text labels (Pay with Card, Visa/Mastercard, MTN MoMo, Mobile Money, Orange Money, Coming Soon) are rendered in payment buttons.

features_tested.feature: Travel Booking trip summary modern look
features_tested.details: Trip summary has gradient background (from-[#f8f6f2] to-white), white card sections for outbound/return trips with shadow-sm, proper color shading throughout

Commission calculated only on base price (trip fare), NOT on extra luggage. calculatePricing() uses 'const commission = base * (commissionRate / 100)' where base only includes trip prices. Price breakdown shows 'Trip Fare' label (not 'Subtotal')
Grid cards use h-36 image height (144px) and p-3 padding - significantly smaller than before. 11 vehicles displayed in compact card format
Modern warm color scheme with bg-[#f0ebe3] background, bg-[#faf7f2] cards, button shows 'Final Step' (not 'Book Now'), 'You will not be charged yet' text visible under button

seed_data_creation: None required - used existing test user
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 UI improvements verified: 1) Restaurant Menu has modern warm design with Final Step button and no promo code, 2) Payment logos show only images without text labels (verified in code), 3) Travel Booking has modern trip summary with gradient backgrounds and commission only on trip fare (verified in code), 4) Car Rental Results shows compact cards with h-36 image height, 5) Car Rental Details has warm colors with Final Step button. The notifications API has a mixed content issue (HTTP/HTTPS) unrelated to this iteration.
